summary: Plan validates existing implementation from US-002. No new files, no new dependencies, no API surface changes. The module is a pure, side-effect-free reader with no persistence or transmission — fully aligned with CLAUDE.md privacy constraints.

risks.description: jsdom mocks for screen, devicePixelRatio, and matchMedia may diverge from real browser behavior, but this is mitigated by explicit Object.defineProperty and vi.spyOn usage in tests

alignmentWithCLAUDEmd.recommendedModuleSplit: src/input/signals.ts matches the recommended path exactly
alignmentWithCLAUDEmd.mappingGuidance: BrowserSignals interface provides all inputs referenced in the mapping table (DPR, timezone, reduced-motion, screen dimensions)
alignmentWithCLAUDEmd.definitionOfDone: plan step 3 covers all five DoD criteria

notes: This is a validation-only pass. The BrowserSignals interface also includes fields beyond the US-003 scope (hardwareConcurrency, prefersColorScheme, prefersReducedMotion, touchCapable) which were implemented proactively in US-002. These extra fields align with CLAUDE.md mapping guidance and do not violate any constraints. No architectural changes needed.
